seoDescription: How Malaysia Property Network scores rental demand, yield, occupancy, liquidity and future growth into a 0–100 investment score.
title: Investment Score Methodology
subtitle: Five dimensions · transparent research · not financial advice
intro: Our 0–100 investment score combines five market dimensions using operational inventory, published transaction bands and district rental benchmarks. Weights vary by project type.

dimensions.title: Rental Demand
dimensions.summary: Tenant pool depth, re-let velocity and cross-border commuter exposure.
dimensions.body: Measures primary tenant segments — local families, Singapore commuters, or overseas owner-management. Mount Austin, Bukit Indah and R&F score highest; Forest City is owner-management weighted with weaker local rental demand.

dimensions.title: Yield
dimensions.summary: Gross rental yield vs entry price and carrying costs.
dimensions.body: Derived from average rent and typical resale entry bands. Danga Bay and Mount Austin show stronger gross yields; Forest City is lower due to entry price and void risk.

dimensions.title: Occupancy
dimensions.summary: Typical void periods and effective occupancy.
dimensions.body: Void weeks converted to effective occupancy. Commuter (R&F) and family (Bukit Indah, Mount Austin) districts show shortest voids; Forest City longest.

dimensions.title: Liquidity
dimensions.summary: Resale volume, listing turnover and mortgage acceptance.
dimensions.body: Mature districts (Danga Bay, Bukit Indah) resell faster; resort-scale Forest City turns slower but suits long-hold owner-management.

dimensions.title: Future Growth
dimensions.summary: Infrastructure, RTS, Iskandar jobs and supply pipeline.
dimensions.body: Medini and Eco Botanic benefit from Iskandar corridor growth; R&F tied to RTS / border policy; Forest City growth narrative is lifestyle-long-term vs local rental.

scoreBands.label: Strong rental investment
scoreBands.desc: High demand, short void, good liquidity
